archetype: Synchrotron Radiation Beamline Experimental Control and Data Integrity System
description: A deterministic real-time control system orchestrating high-precision motorized optical components, vacuum systems, and detectors during sub-second experiments, while ensuring immutable metadata capture and raw data integrity for peer-reviewed publication. Technical hardness stems from nanosecond-level timing synchronization across heterogeneous hardware, radiation-hardened component interfacing, and the need to guarantee no data loss or corruption during multi-hour user beamtime runs.
rules: MUST enforce the EPICS or TANGO control system protocol for device communication and state management.
MUST adhere to the NeXus scientific data format standard for hierarchical, self-describing experimental data.
MUST implement a 'sample environment log' with atomic timestamping from a GPS-disciplined oscillator for absolute temporal correlation across all sensors.
MUST provide a 'data freeze' mechanism that cryptographically hashes the complete dataset and metadata immediately post-run, preventing any subsequent alteration.
MUST integrate with the facility's central accounting system to enforce pre-paid beamtime quotas and automatic experiment termination on quota expiry.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard HTTP/REST for primary device control—its non-deterministic latency violates hard real-time requirements for beam stability.
DO NOT store raw photon event data in a relational database—the write throughput and schema-less nature of binary detector arrays require specialized binary formats or object stores.
DO NOT separate control and data acquisition networks—copper-based control traffic can induce EMI in sensitive analog detector signals, requiring physically isolated, fiber-optic segmented networks.
key_technical_challenges: Achieving sub-100 microsecond closed-loop feedback between a photon counting detector and a piezo-driven mirror to suppress beam jitter during experiments.
Developing a 'misaligned beam' auto-recovery procedure that can safely retract expensive monochromator crystals without human intervention after a vacuum breach.
Implementing a 'data gap' detection and forensic reconstruction system that can identify and flag missing frames caused by network switch buffer overflows during sustained 10 Gb/s data bursts.

recommended_stack.language: Rust or C++ with real-time extensions (e.g., RTAI) for deterministic, memory-safe control loops interfacing with VME/VXI bus hardware.
recommended_stack.framework: EPICS Base (C++) or TANGO (C++/Python) as the established distributed control framework, not a generic web framework.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL) for time-series metadata and experiment logs; object store (Ceph/S3) for immutable raw binary data blobs.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis for in-memory state replication across IOC (Input/Output Controller) nodes within the control system network.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ with PUSH/PULL pattern for low-latency, brokerless data streaming between detector front-ends and aggregation servers.
special_services: EPICS Channel Access Gateway for secure, firewall-traversing client connections
Custom 'NeXus Validator' service that enforces schema compliance pre-ingestion

compliance_flags: IEC 61508 (Functional Safety for high-power magnet/power supply control)
FAIR Data Principles (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) for published datasets
